theme: multi-zone fishery quota setting over a long horizon
title: Tideline Commons: Multi-Zone Fishery Quota Setting
strategies: do-nothing / tiny fixed-rate skim: cull a small constant fraction of the current stock every step, no target level, no collapse reasoning -- safe but leaves most of the sustainable surplus unharvested

static generic-floor greedy: assume one fixed safety buffer (a fraction of carrying capacity) is safe for every zone and harvest everything above it -- underharvests zones whose true max-growth point is higher, and drives some zones straight through their own (higher, hidden) collapse threshold

max-growth-point holding: harvest only the surplus above each zone's own K/2 (the maximum-growth point), which is provably above every zone's guaranteed threshold bound -- captures close to the per-step sustainable-yield ceiling in every zone without ever risking collapse

seeded per-zone tuning / local search: adjust each zone's holding target and warm-up schedule (e.g. from logged trial trajectories) to trade a small collapse-risk margin for extra yield, or to better exploit the finite horizon's endgame
